frameworks.name: Bayes least squares estimation
frameworks.one_line: BLS estimator reformulated without explicit prior, using observed-measurement density.

frameworks.name: nonparametric density estimation
frameworks.one_line: Used to approximate the prior-free BLS estimator from observed data.

frameworks.name: additive Gaussian noise model
frameworks.one_line: Observation model in which a parametric estimator family is derived.

core_question: Can Bayes least squares estimators be formulated and learned without explicit knowledge of the prior?
key_assumptions: Bayesian estimators depend on the posterior, product of likelihood and prior
Uncorrupted samples of the latent variable are unavailable
manipulations: Observation model varied across several noise/measurement processes
Additive Gaussian noise case treated parametrically
key_findings: For many observation models, the BLS estimator can be written directly from the observed-measurement density
The mean squared estimation error admits a related prior-free expression
These forms yield practical nonparametric approximations from observed data alone
A parametric estimator family is derived for additive Gaussian noise
interpretations: Bayesian estimation can be learned without supervision when the prior is unknown
Reformulating in terms of observed-data density makes BLS estimators data-driven and feasible
